title: Spraguea montana
intro: Spraguea montana, also known as Western Spraguea, is a flowering plant native to the western United States and Canada. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Spraguea montana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Spraguea montana is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in topical creams and salves to treat skin conditions and wounds.
